title: On mentoring
snippet: Been mentoring a few engineers more formally over the last six months. Some things that have actually worked:Positive reinforcement is underused. title: Reading in 2026
snippet: Keeping the list open this year. No target number.I’ve noticed that when I’m counting, I optimize for short books. Which is fine, some of the best books are short. But it creates a bias I don’t want.Currently reading Midnight’s Children, which I’ve started and abandoned twice before. Third time s...

title: The Ministry for the Future Review
snippet: One line review - The Ministry for the FutureKim Stanley Robinson writes about climate change the way nobody else does - with actual policy, economics, and the grinding institutional work of trying to change large systems. It’s not a thriller, not a disaster narrative, it’s something more like a ...

title: What building with agents actually looks like
snippet: Eight months since my last post on this. Some things learned from actually building with agents:The reliability problem is more tractable than I expected if you scope tasks tightly. Agents fail on vague instructions for the same reason junior engineers fail on vague instructions. Specificity help...

title: The God of Small Things Review
snippet: One line review - The God of Small ThingsArundhati Roy’s prose is almost too much at times and then suddenly exactly right. The structure - circling the same moment from different distances - should be exhausting but it’s not. The Kerala it describes, the caste and class mechanics operating quiet...

title: Khajuraho
snippet: Flew into Khajuraho on a Thursday. The town exists mostly because of the temples.The Chandela dynasty built around 85 temples here between the 10th and 12th centuries. About 20 survive. The western group is the main draw - Kandariya Mahadeva is the one in every photograph, spires stacked on spire...

title: Hampi
snippet: Fourth year. The drive is almost meditative at this point.Didn’t do much this time. Sat by the Tungabhadra for most of Saturday. Read half a book. Watched a local fisherman work the same stretch of water for two hours with complete focus. Ate at the same place I’ve eaten at for four years.The who...

title: The Art of Doing Science and Engineering Review
snippet: One line review - The Art of Doing Science and EngineeringHamming’s lectures are uneven and repetitive and completely worth reading. He’s really talking about what it means to have a career that matters, not just a job that pays. The chapter “You and Your Research” is available for free online an...

title: Museum of Flight, Seattle
snippet: Was in Seattle for two weeks. Made time for the Museum of Flight toward the end.The place is massive and the collection is serious. But the section I kept coming back to was the World War exhibits. The sheer pace of aviation development compressed into six years is staggering - aircraft went from...

title: On saying no
snippet: Feature requests don’t slow down. They compound. Each one seems reasonable in isolation - small lift, customer value, two days of work. But they accumulate in the system. Each new parameter is a branch. Each configuration option is a test case you’ll miss. Each special case is a concept that futu...

title: Agents are working now
snippet: About a year ago I wrote about the orchestration layer being the interesting problem. It still is, but something has shifted.The agents I was building in early 2024 were brittle. They’d hallucinate tool arguments, lose track of goals mid-task, require too much hand-holding to be useful. Interesti...

title: Pachinko Review
snippet: One line review - PachinkoMin Jin Lee writes four generations of a Korean family in Japan with remarkable patience. No character is a symbol, everyone is just a person trying to get through. The way discrimination shapes lives across generations without ever becoming the explicit point of the boo...

title: On tooling obsession
snippet: I went through a phase a few years back where I spent more time configuring my development environment than actually developing in it. New terminal emulator, custom shell prompt, obscure vim plugins, a particular way of organizing dotfiles that I rewrote three times.The output of that period: a v...

title: An Elegant Puzzle Review
snippet: One line review - An Elegant PuzzleWill Larson writes about engineering management the way someone who has actually done it writes about it - with the specific awkward situations intact, not sanitized. The sections on systems thinking for organizations and on managing technical debt at the team l...

title: Badami and Pattadakal
snippet: Long weekend, flew into Hubli, drove to Badami.This is Chalukya country. The cave temples at Badami are carved into red sandstone cliffs above a green tank. The craftsmanship in the panels - a reclining Vishnu, a dancing Shiva with eighteen arms in perfect count - is precise in a way that feels a...

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications Review
snippet: One line review - Designing Data-Intensive ApplicationsShould have read this five years ago. Kleppmann covers distributed systems in a way that gives you vocabulary for things you’ve observed but couldn’t articulate. The chapters on replication and consensus are worth the price alone. Required re...

title: Hampi
snippet: Third year. Drove down Thursday evening, back Sunday night.At this point the trip has its own rhythm. Same guesthouse near the river, same coracle crossing in the morning, same walk through the bazaar ruins before anyone else is up. The ruins don’t change. I do, apparently, because I keep noticin...

title: The orchestration layer is the interesting problem
snippet: Been spending evenings this past month building small things with LLMs. Most of the discourse is about which model is best, which benchmark, which context window. That’s fine but it’s not the interesting part.The interesting part is orchestration. How do you get an LLM to reliably use tools? How ...

title: Two years at Amazon
snippet: Two years at Amazon now.The scale of systems here is something you adjust to gradually. Your mental model of what a “big” codebase or “large” traffic number keeps getting revised upward. The operational discipline is genuinely different from anywhere I’ve worked before. Runbooks, COEs, the cultur...

title: Ajanta and Ellora
snippet: Aurangabad for four days. Flew in on a Friday.The caves are both necessary and not enough. You need more than two days to really absorb them. Ajanta especially - the Buddhist murals in some of the caves have survived 2000 years and are still recognizable as paintings of real people, real animals,...

title: A Fine Balance Review
snippet: One line review - A Fine BalanceRohinton Mistry does not pull punches. The Emergency-era India in this book is specific and brutal and completely believable. You spend 600 pages caring about these characters and Mistry rewards that investment in the most honest way possible, which is to say, not ...

title: The Remains of the Day Review
snippet: One line review - The Remains of the DayIshiguro writes about self-deception so precisely it’s uncomfortable. Stevens the butler spends the whole novel convincing himself and you that his choices were correct, and by the end you understand exactly how a person can spend a lifetime being excellent...

title: On Rewrites
snippet: Every developer has a rewrite they want to do. A system that grew organically and is now a mess. Edge cases bolted on over years. A module that works but that nobody wants to touch.The argument for a rewrite is always compelling at the start. Clean slate. Right abstractions this time. Modern tool...

title: Hampi, again
snippet: Second year in a row. Drove down again, same route, different headspace.There’s something that happens around the third hour of the drive when the landscape starts to flatten out and the boulders appear. The brain shifts gears whether you want it to or not.This time I spent more time at the Hemak...

title: Project Hail Mary Review
snippet: One line review - Project Hail MaryAndy Weir knows exactly what he’s doing. Same formula as The Martian but I didn’t care. The science problem-solving is genuinely fun and the Rocky storyline is unexpected and earned. Read the last third in one sitting.

title: The job is mostly reading
snippet: Nobody tells you this early enough: the job is mostly reading code, not writing it.I probably spend four hours reading for every one hour I spend writing. Reading to understand what’s already there before touching it. Reading to figure out why a test is failing. Reading to trace a bug through thr...

title: Surely You're Joking, Mr. Feynman Review
snippet: One line review - Surely You’re Joking, Mr. FeynmanAlready knew some of the stories from the internet but reading them in sequence paints a picture of someone who just couldn’t stop being curious about everything. The safe-cracking, the bongo drums, the strip club physics papers. What comes throu...

title: A Philosophy of Software Design Review
snippet: One line review - A Philosophy of Software DesignOusterhout’s central idea - that the primary enemy of good software is complexity, and that shallow modules are worse than deep ones - is useful enough to reshape how you think about API design. title: On Complexity
snippet: The worst thing about complexity in software is that it doesn’t announce itself. It accumulates quietly, one reasonable decision at a time, until one day you’re staring at a pull request and realizing you can’t confidently say what will break if you merge it.I’ve started thinking of it like debt....

title: Hampi
snippet: First Hampi trip. Drove down from Bangalore, about five hours with stops.The scale of the ruins is hard to process. title: Ponniyin Selvan Review

title: Reading Challenge 2022
snippet: Starting the year with Ponniyin Selvan. Have been putting it off for a while.Keeping the target modest, around 15 books. Last year I overcommitted and the quality of reading suffered. Rushing through books to hit a number is pointless.

title: Bye-bye Syncron
snippet: I’m moving on from Syncron after working in Syncron Price. Syncron was a great place to polish my agile development skills. I got to work with multiple people who were very adept in agile methodology and workflow.I will be joining Amazon in the coming days.

title: Leaving Zoho
snippet: I’m moving on from Zoho after 4 years of working with one of their biggest products (Servicedesk Plus). It was a great journey. I learnt a lot and I was able to have a considerable impact in the product itself.The team at Zoho was great at filling gaps for each other, and was held tightly togethe...

title: Animal Farm Review
snippet: One line review - Animal FarmAn amazing book by all standards. Orwell obviously does an amazing job in laying out tough to understand principles in a straight forward manner.Next up, I will be reading Pyre by Perumal Murugan.

title: Ghachar Ghochar Review
snippet: One line review - Ghachar GhocharWhat a great read! A prime example of the literature hidden in other languages.
